_rationale: index_stats mode=symbols: both Go and C++ currently ignore the mode argument and return the standard index_stats payload. Iter-8 (DART-vJrTKPxyk7RF): C++ handler now emits Go-shape body — `timestamp` (RFC3339Nano string) replaces `timestamp_ms` (int), and `total_size_bytes` (sum of FileContentStore content sizes) is now emitted. Both sides produce structurally identical bodies. The parity_runner normalize_mcp_inner_text scrubs single-shot timing residue (status, progress.*, file_count, index_time_ms, reference_count, symbol_count) and the volatile timestamp/total_size_bytes fields — leaving server_ready + structural shape under parity guard. Text body lifted from ignore→stable.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope + inner text body. Mode argument dispatch is locked end-to-end; both binaries treat mode=symbols as a no-op pass-through to the standard payload, and the shape now matches between Go and C++ after iter-8 timestamp/total_size_bytes fix.
tiers._rationale_ignore: Envelope-level non-deterministic fields only — JSON-RPC id mirrors request, jsonrpc is constant, meta.elapsed_ms is wall-clock, meta.server_pid is per-process.
